article_title: pentest-ai-agents – 28 Claude Code Subagents for Penetration Testing
core_thesis: The article introduces pentest-ai-agents, an open-source toolkit that transforms Anthropic's Claude Code into a specialized offensive security research assistant by providing 28 domain-specific subagents covering the full penetration testing lifecycle. It emphasizes ease of installation, a two-tier execution model for safety, persistent findings storage, and compatibility with local models for air-gapped environments, positioning it as a practical force multiplier for red teams.
detailed_summary:
pentest-ai-agents is a new open-source toolkit released by security researcher 0xSteph on GitHub. It consists of 28 Claude Code subagents, each with deep domain expertise across the entire penetration testing lifecycle, including reconnaissance, web application testing, Active Directory attacks, cloud security, mobile pentesting, wireless attacks, social engineering, exploit chaining, detection engineering, forensics, malware analysis, and report generation. Instead of relying on a single general-purpose AI model, the framework automatically routes each query to the most appropriate specialist agent.

Installation is straightforward: a single curl command clones the repository and copies all agent files to ~/.claude/agents/. It is idempotent and supports project-scoped deployments and a lite mode that uses Claude Haiku for advisory agents to reduce token costs. The toolkit introduces a two-tier execution model: Tier 1 agents operate in advisory mode (users paste tool output and receive analysis and recommendations), while Tier 2 agents can compose and execute commands directly against an authorized scope, with each command requiring explicit user approval. Tier 2 agents include Recon Advisor, Web Hunter, AD Attacker, Exploit Chainer, PoC Validator, and Business Logic Hunter, with all offensive actions mapped to MITRE ATT&CK identifiers.

A built-in SQLite-backed findings database persists engagement data across sessions, enabling multi-day operations. The Report Generator agent produces professional pentest reports with executive summaries, CVSS scoring, and remediation roadmaps. For air-gapped environments, agents can be converted to OpenCode custom commands compatible with local models via an included script. A companion MCP server extends the ecosystem with 150+ tool wrappers, autonomous exploit chaining, and CI/CD pipeline integration for Claude Desktop, Cursor, and VS Code Copilot.

entities.name: 0xSteph
entities.role: Security researcher who released pentest-ai-agents on GitHub

entities.name: Anthropic
entities.role: Creator of Claude Code, the AI model that pentest-ai-agents extends

entities.name: Claude Code
entities.role: AI coding assistant from Anthropic, used as the base for the subagents

entities.name: MITRE ATT&CK
entities.role: Framework used to map offensive actions to known adversary tactics and techniques

key_data_points: The toolkit includes 28 domain-specific subagents covering the full penetration testing lifecycle, from reconnaissance to report generation (🟢 relevant to your Red Teaming: provides specialized AI assistance for every phase of an engagement, reducing manual effort).

Installation is a single curl command and is idempotent; it supports a lite mode that uses Claude Haiku for advisory agents to reduce token consumption (🟢 relevant to your Homelab/DevOps: easy setup with cost optimization options for resource-constrained environments).

Two-tier execution model: Tier 1 agents provide advisory analysis (user pastes tool output), while Tier 2 agents can execute commands directly against an authorized scope with explicit user approval (🟢 relevant to your Red Teaming: balances automation with safety, allowing you to automate command execution while maintaining control).

Tier 2 agents include Recon Advisor (nmap, whois, whatweb), Web Hunter (ffuf, sqlmap, dalfox), AD Attacker (BloodHound, Impacket, CrackMapExec, Certipy), Exploit Chainer, PoC Validator, and Business Logic Hunter (🟢 relevant to your Red Teaming: covers key offensive tools and techniques, enabling automated chaining of exploits).

All offensive actions are mapped to MITRE ATT&CK identifiers and paired with defensive context (🟢 relevant to your Red Teaming: helps align your actions with industry-standard frameworks and understand defensive countermeasures).

A built-in SQLite-backed findings database persists engagement data across sessions, enabling multi-day operations with seamless handoffs (🟢 relevant to your Red Teaming: supports long-term engagements and collaborative work).

The Report Generator agent produces professional pentest reports with executive summaries, CVSS scoring, and remediation roadmaps (🟢 relevant to your Red Teaming: automates the often tedious report writing process, ensuring consistent and professional deliverables).

Agents can be converted to OpenCode custom commands compatible with Ollama, LM Studio, or any local model via the included opencode-setup.sh script (🟢 relevant to your Homelab/DevOps: enables air-gapped or privacy-sensitive operations using local LLMs).

A companion MCP server provides 150+ tool wrappers, autonomous exploit chaining, and CI/CD pipeline integration for Claude Desktop, Cursor, and VS Code Copilot (🟢 relevant to your Red Teaming and DevOps: extends automation into development workflows and integrates with popular IDEs).
methodology_steps: Install pentest-ai-agents by running: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/0xSteph/pentest-ai-agents/main/install.sh | bash. This clones the repository and copies all 28 agent files to ~/.claude/agents/. The script is idempotent, so re-running updates existing agents.
Optionally, use --project for project-scoped deployments or --global --lite for a cost-optimized lite mode that runs advisory agents on Claude Haiku.
Use Tier 1 agents by pasting tool output (e.g., from nmap, whois) into Claude Code; the agent will provide prioritized analysis, methodology guidance, and recommended next commands.

For Tier 2 agents, declare an authorized scope and allow the agent to compose and execute commands directly. Claude Code will display each command for explicit approval before execution. Tier 2 agents include Recon Advisor, Web Hunter, AD Attacker, Exploit Chainer, PoC Validator, and Business Logic Hunter.
To persist findings across sessions, ensure findings.sh is in the system PATH. Tier 2 agents will automatically write to the SQLite-backed findings database.
Generate professional pentest reports using the Report Generator agent, which produces executive summaries, CVSS scoring, and remediation roadmaps.
For air-gapped environments, run the included opencode-setup.sh script to convert agents to OpenCode custom commands compatible with Ollama, LM Studio, or any local model.
Integrate with Claude Desktop, Cursor, or VS Code Copilot using the companion MCP server (pentest-ai), which provides 150+ tool wrappers and autonomous exploit chaining.
conclusions: pentest-ai-agents provides a practical, modular AI assistant for penetration testing that can significantly accelerate red team workflows by routing queries to specialized subagents. The two-tier execution model balances automation with safety, making it suitable for both advisory and active testing scenarios. The toolkit's support for local models and air-gapped environments ensures it can be used in sensitive or offline settings, which is critical for many professional engagements.

The inclusion of a persistent findings database and automated report generation addresses common pain points in penetration testing, such as data continuity across sessions and the time-consuming nature of report writing. By mapping actions to MITRE ATT&CK, the toolkit also helps red teams align with industry standards and communicate findings effectively.

For red teamers and security researchers, pentest-ai-agents represents a significant step toward integrating AI into offensive security operations without sacrificing control or requiring complex infrastructure. Its open-source nature and active development suggest it will continue to evolve with community contributions.
personal_relevance_summary: This article is highly relevant to your Red Teaming and programming interests. The pentest-ai-agents toolkit provides 28 specialized AI subagents that can automate and enhance every phase of a penetration test, from reconnaissance to report generation. As a programmer, you can leverage the open-source code to customize agents or integrate them into your own tooling. The two-tier execution model allows you to maintain control over command execution while benefiting from AI-driven analysis, and the support for local models means you can run it in your homelab without cloud dependencies. The persistent findings database and automated report generation will save you time on documentation, allowing you to focus on more complex technical challenges. Additionally, the MCP server integration with IDEs like VS Code means you can incorporate AI-assisted security analysis directly into your development workflow.
extra_advice: Consider forking the repository and extending it with custom subagents for your specific red teaming needs, such as a subagent specialized in cloud-specific attacks (e.g., AWS IAM enumeration, GCP privilege escalation) or one that integrates with your favorite C2 framework. You could also contribute back to the project by adding support for additional tools or creating a subagent that automates the generation of custom payloads based on the findings database. Given your interest in homelabbing, set up a local LLM (e.g., via Ollama) and test the OpenCode conversion to run the entire toolkit offline—this will give you a fully air-gapped AI red team assistant.
